item.title: First Principles: Distributed Systems (Most of Yours Should Not Exist)
item.description: Distributed systems thinking is drowning in inherited wisdom — microservices by default, horizontal scaling as a reflex, eventual consistency as a catchphrase. Strip it down to six irreducible truths and most of that architectural baggage falls away. The uncomfortable conclusion: the first, cheapest, most reliable distributed system is the one you did not build.
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio

item.title: First Principles: Data Engineering and ETLs
item.description: ETL is not a law of nature, it is a convention born from constraints that no longer exist. Strip data engineering down to its irreducible truths and rebuild it from scratch. What emerges is a discipline centered on contracts and observability, not pipelines and orchestrators.
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio

item.title: First Principles: DevOps
item.description: Strip away the Kubernetes hype, the “DevOps Engineer” job titles, and the cargo-culted CI/CD pipelines. What’s actually true about DevOps when you reason from first principles? Five irreducible truths, six design principles, and a clear framework for right-sizing your operational complexity.
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio

item.title: First Principles: Software Design
item.description: Strip away the design patterns, the SOLID principles, and the architecture astronautics. What’s actually true about software design when you reason from fundamentals? Five irreducible truths, three reconstruction principles, and a framework for making design decisions based on cognitive load, not convention.
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio

item.title: First Principles: Software Observability
item.description: Everyone knows observability means logs, metrics, and traces. But what if you strip away the vendor marketing, the inherited conventions, and the cargo-culted dashboards? What’s actually true? A first-principles deconstruction of software observability.
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio

item.title: Taming Your Dev Chaos with direnv, .env, and .envrc (Plus a Sneak Peek at mise-en-place)
item.description: Stop manually juggling env vars like a clown — learn how direnv auto-magically handles your dev environments with .env and .envrc files. Includes a sick custom layout uv function for Python projects and a teaser on mise-en-place, the dev tool that does it all.
item.dc:creator: Alexandre Manhães Savio

item.title: Git usage guidelines for team collaboration
item.description: Suggestions on how to use a code repository with git for teams.
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio

item.title: Remove big files from a git repository
item.description: Remove large blobs from your git repository history
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio

item.title: Access Google Spreadsheets using gspread and OAuth2Client
item.description: How to access Google spreadsheets using the Python module gspread and the OAuth2Client authorization system.
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio

item.title: Install Bokeh from sources
item.description: A short guide to install Bokeh from sources in a virtualenv on Ubuntu 14.04
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio

item.title: How to use Byobu
item.description: A short manual for Byobu and a list of its advantages.
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio

item.title: How to set up different versions of GCC
item.description: How to set up different versions of GCC
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio

item.title: Installing common Python data analysis tools in virtualenv
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio

item.title: Compile Numpy and Scipy against Intel MKL
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio

item.title: Using BibLatex with Lyx and JabRef
item.description: Configuring BibLatex in Lyx to benefit of UTF-8 encoding
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio

item.title: How to create virtualenvs with Virtualenvwrapper
item.dc:creator: Alexandre M. Savio
